---
title: "LRP68: Does word reading route through taught-expressive vocabulary? (mediation)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

The 2026-07-10 DAG revision added the direct edges $TE \to WR$ and $EV \to WR$ (decision 5)
precisely so estimands conditioning on the phonics gateway no longer force the non-phonemic
*expressive* route to zero by construction. LRP68 exercises that edge: a single-mediator
g-formula decomposition of the intervention's word-reading ($W$) effect through
**taught-expressive vocabulary $TE$** — the whole-word / paired-associate route, motivated
within DS by Mengoni, Nash & Hulme (2014). $TE$ (the bespoke taught words) is a more proximal
product of this whole-word programme than the standardised $E$ used in
[LRP64](../lrp-rli-med-064).

## Design

Phase 0 only, $n \approx 53$. Mediator $TE_{t2}$ (Beta-Binomial on $TE_{t1}$); outcome
$W_{t2}$ with a $G\times TE$ interaction. Adjustment $\{G, A, L, R, W_\text{pre}, TE_{t1}\}$
— letter-sound $L$ (the competing code route) and receptive vocab $R$ are the measured
mediator–outcome confounders. Standardised $E$ is a *descendant* of $TE$ (transfer edge
$TE \to EV$), so it is deliberately **not** adjusted (over-control).

## Assumptions

ID-2: the $TE \to WR$ leg is $GA$-confounded and dose is a treatment-induced
mediator–outcome confounder, so the NDE/NIE are **adjusted associations, not
point-identified**. Contemporaneous measurement; $n \approx 53 \Rightarrow$ wide intervals.

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_mediation.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
